tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Fall is all about topwater. Use a Sexy Dawg to cast to surfacing activity — the Hard Knock version so bass hear it too. 7-4 MH fast tip rod, 40-50lb braid for optimum retrieve. Best shad activity often comes in the afternoon in fall.'
KVD: 'Bass can be finicky about baitfish size in fall — downsize to a KVD 1.0 squarebill. That smaller bait can be phenomenal.'
KVD: 'In fall I often downsize to a 3/8 or 5/16oz Finesse spinnerbait with smaller blades to match baitfish size. Target channel-swing banks, mud flats, and backs of creeks where shad hang out.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'I look for a 10-degree drop from average summer water temps to trigger the fall pattern. On Ozarks reservoirs that's when temps drop from low-mid 80s to mid 70s. Length of day is the most important determinant — shorter days trigger change.'
KVD: 'You don't have to be out at dawn in fall. The best shad activity often comes in the afternoon — especially later into fall, the shad seem more active when it warms up a bit.'
KVD: 'It isn't always shad. In northern lakes, bluegills are often the main prey. In the southeast, blueback herring are dominant. Know the habits of the dominant prey species to be successful.'
